figures.caption: Table 2: Diversity metrics at different retrieval depths (𝑘) using hybrid retrieval. Opinion-Enriched KB consistently outperforms Raw Discussions KB on scored diversity metrics while showing trade-offs on categorical coverage. Statistical significance via Wilcoxon signed-rank test (𝑛 = 48): bold = p<0.05, bold* = p<0.001.

Table 8: Side-by-side comparison. The Raw KB organizes by action categories and admits “search results don’t show clear feedback” on outcomes. The Opinion-Enriched KB captures sentiment distribution (mixed but positive), success attribution (only ones who helped), behavioral explanations (rarely post, last resort), and constructive suggestions missing from Raw KB.

Table 6: Complete analysis of major RAG/LLM evaluation datasets (2013-2025). All high-impact benchmarks prioritize factual accuracy over opinion synthesis, with only TruthfulQA and OpinioRAG showing partial opinion-awareness.

Figure 1: Opinion-Aware RAG Architecture. Traditional RAG (bottom, grayed) indexes raw documents directly. Our approach introduces an opinion enrichment step (highlighted) that extracts per-entity opinion and author attributes before indexing.
